dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7899 IH: Respect for Essential Workers Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Cherfilus-McCormick introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To limit the removal from the United States of certain recipients of temporary protected status who are essential workers, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Respect for Essential Workers Act.

section.header: Temporary Protected Status for essential workers

subsection.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a covered alien may not be removed from the United States on the basis that the grant of temporary protected status under section 244 of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1254a) has been terminated.

Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a covered alien may adjust to the status of an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence under section 245 of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1255) beginning on the date that is 90 days after the date of enactment of this Act.

paragraph.text: The term covered alien means an alien who—

subparagraph.text: on the date of enactment of this Act, is present in the United States pursuant to a grant of temporary protected status under section 244 of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1254a); and
works in or receives training for an occupation in—

clause.text: an essential industry or in a region with a documented labor shortage, as determined by the Secretary of Labor; or
a health care-related profession in a health professional shortage area, as determined by the Administrator of the Health Resources and Services Administration.

paragraph.text: The term essential industry means any of the following:

subparagraph.text: Restaurant ownership, food preparation, vending, catering, food packaging, food services, or food delivery.
Agricultural work, including labor that is seasonal in nature.
Housing, residential, or commercial construction-related activities, or public works construction.
Natural disaster recovery, disaster reconstruction, and related construction.

clause.text: the provision of assistance with activities of daily living;

any service provided by a direct care worker (as such term is defined in section 799B of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 295p)), a personal care aide, job coach, or supported employment provider; and
the provision of any other type of care to an individual in their home by a direct service provider, personal care attendant, and home health aide.

subparagraph.text: Family care, including the provision of child care services, including in-home child care services (such as nanny services), and care services provided by one family member to another family member.
